headline: Singapore charged a fourth in the Nvidia chip case. No Malaysian operator faces charges yet.
context: Singapore charged three men in Feb 2025 over servers that, per the Home Affairs Minister, possibly carried Nvidia AI chips routed to Malaysia. A fourth was charged in April 2026. Malaysia imposed a Strategic Trade Permit on US AI chips on 14 July 2025. No Malaysian operator has been publicly named.

cards.big: Singapore's chip case grew to four people charged: three on 27 Feb 2025 (a Singaporean, a permanent resident, a Chinese national), a fourth on 2 April 2026.
cards.sub: Singapore's Minister Shanmugam said the servers possibly carried Nvidia chips and moved to Malaysia; the final destination was unknown.

cards.big: The US 'Tier 2' label came from a rule that never officially named Malaysia and was paused before it took effect.
cards.sub: BIS issued the AI Diffusion Rule on 13 Jan 2025, announced rescission 13 May 2025. GAO ruled in May 2026 the rule still stood until rulemaking completes.

cards.big: Johor approved 51 data-centre projects worth RM183 billion; 17 operational. In November 2025 the state stopped approving lower-tier DCs.
cards.sub: Planned DCs could absorb over half Johor's treated water. YTL, ByteDance and GDS have Johor footprints.

cards.big: Malaysia made US-origin AI chips subject to a Strategic Trade Permit on 14 July 2025. MITI said in March 2025 it had no findings of Nvidia chips reaching Malaysia.
cards.sub: The Strategic Trade Act 2010 caps unlicensed-export fines at RM10M (RM20M with intent). No Malaysian operator faces public charges.

cards.big: The permit exists. Bridge changed tenants. Johor tightened approvals. The test isn't whether anyone acted. The test is whether anyone gets named.
Like writing the building code after the towers go up. Now everyone watches which ones get inspected.
cards.sub: Malaysia wrote its US-chip permit in July 2025, five months after the Singapore charges. Bridge Data Centres swapped a Chinese tenant in February 2026.

cards.big: Singapore charged four. Malaysia wrote a permit. The market swapped a tenant. No Malaysian operator has been publicly named for the case that triggered all of it.
